stem:
The table below shows selected values of the linear function $g$.

| $x$ | $g(x)$ |
|-----|--------|
| $1$ | $9$    |
| $3$ | $15$   |
| $5$ | $21$   |

What is the $y$-intercept of the graph of $g$ in the $xy$-plane?
explanation:
First, find the slope using any two points from the table. Using $(1, 9)$ and $(3, 15)$:
$$m = \frac{15 - 9}{3 - 1} = \frac{6}{2} = 3$$

Next, use the slope and one point to find the y-intercept $b$ in $g(x) = 3x + b$. Substituting the point $(1, 9)$:
$$9 = 3(1) + b$$
$$9 = 3 + b$$
$$b = 6$$

The y-intercept of the graph of $g$ is $6$. This can be verified: $g(0) = 3(0) + 6 = 6$.

distractorRationale.A: Reads the slope $m = 3$ as the y-intercept instead of computing $g(0)$ using the slope-intercept form.
distractorRationale.B: Correct. The slope is $\frac{15-9}{3-1} = 3$, and substituting $(1, 9)$ gives $9 = 3(1) + b$, so $b = 6$.
distractorRationale.C: Uses the value $g(1) = 9$ as the y-intercept, mistakenly treating the function value at $x = 1$ as the value at $x = 0$.
distractorRationale.D: Extends the pattern in the wrong direction by adding the slope to $g(1)$ instead of subtracting: $9 + 3 = 12$ instead of $9 - 3 = 6$.
